fault_hypothesis: Line 81 uses a simple `int(v)` (floor/truncation) conversion which loses fractional remainders, so the sum of integer allocations does not equal the total when weights don't divide evenly. A largest-remainder method should be used instead.

spec.expr: sum(int(v) for v in shares__1) == total

constraints.intent: After computing integer allocations via int(), their sum must equal total — violated when fractional parts are simply truncated.
Raw shares must sum to total before integer rounding is applied.
The integer-rounded result list must sum exactly to the integer total.
When floor_to_int is True, the returned allocations must sum to total.
